title: Tetrachyron websteri
intro: Tetrachyron websteri is a flowering plant species of the family Apiaceae, commonly known as a type of wild celery or wild parsnip. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including rheumatism and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Tetrachyron websteri include sesquiterpenes, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In herbal preparations, Tetrachyron websteri is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, indigestion, and other conditions.
